A book in 114 chapters · May 2026

The Harness Engineering Book

Deep-dives on building reliable AI agents — from the agent loop to the frontier of test-time scaling. Written for engineers, explained for everyone.

114
Chapters
7
Parts
~12h
Reading time
~280k
Words
🎭
Real-world analogies. Each chapter opens with a story you already know — a chef during dinner rush, a chess engine, a bouncer at the door — to make the abstract concrete.
🧒
Plain English mode. Toggle "ELI5" in the sidebar and every chapter gets a metaphor-led explanation up top, no jargon.
📊
Live diagrams. Mermaid flowcharts visualize agent loops, multi-agent flows, tournaments, decision trees, and more — animated, themed, dark-mode-aware.
🔢
Key-numbers cards. Big visual stat tiles surface the impactful metrics — "+23% on SWE-Bench", "98% cost reduction", "16× parallel rollouts".
🅰
Glossary tooltips. Hover any technical term — RAG, MCP, KV cache, PRM, RTV — for an instant definition. Tap to pin on touch devices.
🔎
Line-by-line code walkthroughs. The pseudo-code blocks on the core chapters are paired with a plain-English panel: what each line does, a worked example, and the one thing to remember.
📖
Reads like a book. Sidebar TOC, prev/next nav, reading progress, bookmarks, dark mode — all saved locally.
Sourced from `harness-engineering/docs/` · Each chapter cites primary papers, blogs, and code · Last built · build
